skill.name: smart-bug-fix
skill.description: Guides systematic bug fixing with root cause analysis, Codex-assisted implementation, multi-model review, and validation checks.
skill.summary: Fix bugs through RCA, automated implementation, and regression validation.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command-related patterns, but they are in Markdown workflow examples rather than packaged executable scripts. The external command workflow is still a real medium-risk concern because it asks agents to run CLIs, inspect files, and apply automated fixes to user-selected code.

medium_findings.title: External Command Workflow
medium_findings.description: The documented workflow invokes npx claude-flow, gemini, codex, find, xargs, jq, and other shell commands using the supplied bug description and context path. This appears aligned with the debugging purpose, but it can inspect local files and modify code if followed by an agent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell block explicitly runs external tools and includes Codex full-auto fix behavior. The commands are documented examples, not bundled executable files, so the risk is medium rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Automated Code Modification Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill directs Codex to implement fixes automatically and output a fix implementation directory. This is legitimate for a bug-fix skill, but it can change project files and should be used only in trusted repositories with review and tests.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly request automated fixing and iterative validation. No malicious payload or hidden exfiltration was found, but the behavioral impact is material.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography matches occur in prose, YAML contracts, headings, comments, or example commands. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic API being used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines describe bug-fix inputs, command names, headings, or simple JavaScript conditionals. There is no cryptographic operation in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance matches point to model and workflow descriptions, not host discovery commands. No evidence found of uname, whoami, hostname, id, network interface discovery, or similar system inventory behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines mention Codex roles in the workflow. They do not invoke system reconnaissance commands or request collection of host metadata.

low_findings.title: Command Substitution Used for Local Summaries
low_findings.description: Shell substitutions read generated reports and test outputs to print summaries. This is not malicious, but malformed or unexpected files could affect terminal output if the example script is used directly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The substitutions are visible and limited to local workflow files. They do not execute remote payloads, but they are still shell behavior that deserves caution.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow invokes local and third-party CLIs for analysis, implementation, and validation. Users should review commands before execution and run them in a controlled workspace.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command invocations are explicit in the Markdown shell example. They are purpose-aligned, but they create real execution and file-access exposure.

content.user_title: Fix Bugs with RCA and Test Validation
content.value_statement: Debugging complex failures can waste engineering time and miss regressions. This skill guides RCA, Codex-assisted fixes, and validation checks.
actual_capabilities: Structures bug reports into root cause, fix, validation, and confidence sections.
Guides use of RCA, Codex, and validation agents in one debugging workflow.
Shows how to run context analysis for larger JavaScript and TypeScript codebases.
Defines a test iteration loop with a maximum of five fix attempts.
Includes failure modes for inconclusive RCA, failed tests, regressions, and performance issues.
limitations: Requires external tools such as claude-flow, Codex, Gemini, jq, and shell utilities.
Does not include executable source code beyond documented command examples.
Needs human review before accepting generated fixes in production code.
Best suited for repositories with existing tests and clear reproduction details.

use_cases.title: Investigate a Product Bug
use_cases.description: Turn a user-reported failure into a root cause, candidate fix, and validation checklist.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Incident Follow-up
use_cases.description: Analyze a production defect after mitigation and verify that the final fix does not regress behavior.

use_cases.title: Review Automated Fixes
use_cases.description: Compare a Codex-generated patch against RCA evidence, tests, and performance impact before merge.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Bug Fix
prompt_templates.scenario: A clear bug has a known file path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use smart-bug-fix for this bug: [describe failure]. Context path: [file or folder]. Provide root cause, fix plan, tests, and confidence.

prompt_templates.title: Bug With Reproduction Steps
prompt_templates.scenario: The bug can be reproduced by a short manual flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use smart-bug-fix on [context path]. Bug: [description]. Reproduction steps: [steps]. Include evidence, affected files, and regression checks.

prompt_templates.title: Bug With Logs
prompt_templates.scenario: The bug has stack traces or runtime logs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the smart-bug-fix workflow for [bug]. Context path: [path]. Error logs: [paste relevant logs]. Focus on root cause and test validation.

prompt_templates.title: Deep Multi-Model Review
prompt_templates.scenario: The issue spans a large or risky codebase.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use smart-bug-fix in deep mode for [bug]. Analyze alternatives, apply only reviewed changes, validate tests, check regressions, and report performance impact.

output_examples.input: API requests time out under load in the service layer.
output: Root cause points to connection pool exhaustion during concurrent requests.
Fix plan adjusts pooling and timeout handling, then adds load-focused tests.
Validation reports passing tests, no detected regressions, and low performance risk.

output_examples.input: Login fails in Firefox after the recent auth refactor.
output: Evidence narrows the failure to browser-specific cookie handling.
The proposed change restores compatible cookie attributes and updates auth tests.
The final report lists changed files, validation status, and remaining manual checks.

best_practices: Run the workflow in a branch or disposable workspace before applying changes.
Provide reproduction steps, logs, and the smallest useful context path.
Review every generated patch and run the project test suite before merge.
anti_patterns: Using the workflow on untrusted repositories without reviewing shell commands.
Accepting automated fixes when RCA evidence is weak or tests are missing.
Providing broad filesystem paths when a focused module path is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically fix code?
faq.answer: It documents a workflow that can use Codex auto-fix. Users should review all changes before applying them.

faq.question: Which tools does it expect?
faq.answer: The examples reference claude-flow, Codex, Gemini, jq, find, xargs, and common shell utilities.

faq.question: Is it safe for production incidents?
faq.answer: It can support incident follow-up, but fixes should be tested and reviewed before production deployment.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide a bug description, context path, reproduction steps, and relevant error logs.

faq.question: Does it work without tests?
faq.answer: It can still guide analysis, but confidence is lower without automated or manual validation.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes shell command examples and automated code modification guidance, which require user oversight.
